result:
Pre-registration complete — `runs/pending_experiment.json` is written and figcheck passes. Nothing outside `runs/` was touched; the harness will now implement, train, and score it.

## Experiment 30: supervised lighting dispatcher with day/dusk/night specialist heads

**Why this pivot.** The last four reverted experiments (26–29) all attacked capacity or readout width, so per the plateau rule I left that family entirely and opened the one design family §3 explicitly invites but the history has never tried: dispatcher + lighting-condition specialists. The champion's evidence points there once you correct for the coverage–median coupling: per-bucket medians look flat, but that flatness is manufactured by abstention — night coverage sits at 0.25–0.31 versus 0.5–0.67 for daytime, and the binding §6 cell is Frankfurt *evening*. The model copes with low light by refusing to answer, not by localizing. Notably, exp 12 — the only head-side change kept since exp 16 — was a deliberately crippled mini-version of exactly this idea (a texture-only dark expert with an unsupervised gate), and it still won 8%.

**The one change.** Replace the two-expert gated blend with three *full* layout-aware specialist heads (560→1024 each) for day {morning, midday, afternoon}, dusk {early_evening, evening}, and night, mixed by a tiny 3-way dispatcher that reads the crop's brightness plus its 48-d texture descriptor. The dispatcher is trained *supervised* — the lighting bucket of every training crop is known for free, so the routing gets a direct cross-entropy signal (weight 0.3) instead of exp 12's indirect gradient. Everything downstream (C4 vote, sharpened decode, confidence, calibration, training data and schedule) is untouched.

**Paying for it.** Three full heads are ~6.9 MB in fp32, over the 4 MiB gate — so the export reuses the int8 FC-quantization recipe that exps 26/29 mechanically proved passes the gates at ~7 m accuracy cost (`quantize_dynamic` on MatMul/Gemm only; convs stay fp32 since ConvInteger is fatal). Expected artifact: ~2.5–2.7 MB.

**Pre-registered prediction.** If the lighting-shared template table is binding, night/evening cells improve most, night coverage rises at the unchanged 40% calibration floor, and the worst-case drops from 797.91 m to ~700–760 m. A tie with healthy routing accuracy would cleanly refute regime-conditioning — a *different* refutation than the capacity family, since parameters per applied readout are unchanged. I also pre-registered the coverage-drop confound so a fake median win can't slip through.

The implementation brief spells out all seven file-level changes (model.py head/dispatcher/loss/export, train.py regime threading) with the fixed CLI and ONNX contracts restated, plus sanity numbers (~1.92 M params, dispatcher CE collapsing early is expected behavior).
